title: Equivalent MACRO definitions yield different results
description:
### What happens?

It is my understanding that
```sql
CREATE MACRO s2(x) AS (sum(x) OVER());
```
and
```sql
CREATE MACRO s1(x) AS (SELECT sum(y) OVER() FROM (SELECT x AS y));
```
should yield identical results. They produce, however, different results.

If `s1()` is for some reason not allowed, it should at least issue an error instead of providing a wrong result.


In this example it is easy to workaround (by choosing `s2()` instead of `s1()`). But when using it in more complex context, such a subquery construction might become necessary to e.g. avoid a "nested window functions not allowed" error.

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE tab (x int);

INSERT INTO tab VALUES (3);
INSERT INTO tab VALUES (7);

CREATE MACRO s1(x) AS (SELECT sum(y) OVER() FROM (SELECT x AS y));
CREATE MACRO s2(x) AS (sum(x) OVER());

SELECT s1(x), s2(x) FROM tab;
```

Output:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ s1(x) в”Ҷ s2(x) в”Ӯ
в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
в”Ӯ     3 в”Ҷ    10 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ     7 в”Ҷ    10 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

poc: SELECT sum(y) OVER() FROM (SELECT x AS y));
SELECT s1(x), (SELECT sum(y) OVER() FROM (SELECT x AS y)) AS expanded from tab;
SELECT s2(x), sum(x) over () AS expanded FROM tab;
select (SELECT sum(y) OVER() FROM (SELECT x AS y) t) from tab;
SELECT x, (SELECT SUM(x) FROM tab) AS full_subquery from tab;
key_bug_features: Different results from equivalent MACRO definitions
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tab2 (x int, y float, z varchar(10));
CREATE MACRO s3(x) AS (SELECT sum(y) OVER() FROM (SELECT x AS y));
SELECT s3(x), sum(x) OVER() FROM tab2;
